L1.area: Custom SQL extension and callback registration APIs

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the auxiliary data API for user-defined SQL functions. The API returns direct references to cached data that may be freed by the underlying database engine at any time, allowing callers to access deallocated memory.

L3.area: Auxiliary data caching within the user-defined scalar function interface
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the auxiliary data caching API for user-defined SQL functions. The getter method returns a direct borrow of cached data that may be reclaimed by the SQLite engine between calls, allowing callers to hold a reference to freed memory.

ground_truth.reason: The Auxdata API's getter returns a direct reference (&T) to data managed by SQLite's sqlite3_get_auxdata, but SQLite can free this data at any time, resulting in a use-after-free when the caller accesses the dangling reference.
